summary: Auth persistence bug fix verified. All tests pass - login works, user stays on dashboard, no error toasts, subscription plan changes work without 401 errors.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/auth/login returns valid cookies (access_token, refresh_token) - PASS
Backend: GET /api/auth/me with valid cookie returns user data - PASS
Backend: POST /api/auth/refresh with valid refresh cookie returns new token - PASS
Backend: GET /api/auth/me without cookie returns 401 - PASS
Backend: POST /api/auth/refresh without cookie returns 401 - PASS
Backend: Driver login (driver@test.com / driver123) succeeds - PASS
Backend: Driver can access deliveries after login - PASS
Backend: POST /api/subscription/update requires auth (401 without) - PASS
Backend: POST /api/subscription/update with auth succeeds - PASS
Backend: GET /api/subscription/current with auth returns plan - PASS
Backend: GET /api/dashboard/stats with auth succeeds - PASS
Backend: GET /api/dashboard/cash-flow with auth succeeds - PASS
Backend: Full auth flow (login -> me -> refresh -> me -> protected endpoint) - PASS
Frontend: Login as admin (admin@transporter-pro.com / admin123) - dashboard loads WITHOUT error toasts - PASS
Frontend: After login, user stays on /dashboard - NO redirect to /login - PASS
Frontend: Dashboard data loads correctly (stats, deliveries, sidebar, cash-flow) - PASS
Frontend: Navigate between tabs (Vue d'ensemble, Livraisons, Cash-Flow, Litiges, Abonnement) - no auth errors - PASS
Frontend: Change subscription plan (Croissance, Flotte Pro, Solo) - no 401 errors, success toasts appear - PASS
Frontend: Visit /dashboard when not logged in - spinner shown, then redirect to /login - PASS
Frontend: Login as driver (driver@test.com / driver123) - driver dashboard loads correctly - PASS
Frontend: No console errors about 401 or authentication failures during normal use - PASS
critical_code_review_comments: AuthGate component correctly blocks rendering until loading=false - prevents race condition
AuthContext.checkAuth() correctly tries token refresh on 401 before setting user=false
Axios 401 interceptor correctly does NOT redirect - only refreshes and retries, lets React Router handle auth
SubscriptionContext.updatePlan no longer does window.location redirect
All window.location.href='/login' redirects removed from frontend code
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth persistence bug fix verified in iteration 8. The fix removed window.location.href='/login' from axios 401 interceptor and added AuthGate component to block rendering until auth state is confirmed. AuthContext now tries token refresh before declaring user not authenticated. All subscription plan changes work without 401 errors. Firestore permission errors in console are non-blocking and expected in preview environment.

mocked_apis.firestore_sync: May fail with Permission Denied in preview environment (non-blocking)

bug_fix_verification.original_issue: App was redirecting to login immediately after logging in on mobile. 'Erreur lors du chargement des données' toast appeared and user was kicked to login.
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: axios 401 interceptor was doing window.location.href='/login' on refresh failure, causing race condition with initial data loading
fix_applied: AuthGate component wraps everything, blocks rendering until auth state confirmed
AuthContext tries token refresh before declaring user not authenticated
Axios interceptor no longer redirects (React Router handles it)
Removed all window.location.href redirects from SubscriptionContext and SubscriptionPage
bug_fix_verification.verification_status: FIXED - All tests pass, no 401 errors, user stays on dashboard after login
